title: Fastest Way to Pay Off Credit Cards — TheDailyo
h1: Fastest Way to Pay Off Credit Cards
subtitle: Model aggressive payoff scenarios and compare strategy options.
description: Calculate payoff time and interest with larger payments, extra payments, and lower APR scenarios to find the fastest way to pay off credit cards.
howItWorksHtml:
The fastest payoff is usually a combination of higher payments and lower interest. This page lets you model an aggressive payment plan (including extra payments) so you can see how payoff time and interest change.

Tip: Try increasing your monthly payment first, then experiment with an extra amount to see what moves the timeline the most.

faq.q: What usually makes the biggest difference: extra payments or a lower APR?
faq.a: Both help, but lowering APR can reduce interest every month, while higher payments reduce principal faster. For many people, a combination works best—pay more while also trying to reduce the rate.

faq.q: Is it better to pay extra monthly or make a one-time lump sum?
faq.a: A lump sum reduces principal immediately, which can reduce interest right away. Extra monthly payments add consistency. You can approximate a lump sum by temporarily increasing the payment and comparing outcomes.

faq.q: What if I keep using the card while paying it off?
faq.a: This model assumes no new charges. If you keep adding charges, payoff time can increase significantly. For a clean plan, stop new spending on the card while you pay it down.

affiliate.headline: Lower your APR and pay off faster
